cont_nlp.text:
AGI - Iran has launched over 200 drones and missiles against military targets on Israeli territory in response to the Tel Aviv attack in Damascus that on April 1 caused the death of some high officials of the Guardians of the Revolution. The Jewish state promised an "unparalleled response" and reported that 99% of the bullets were intercepted, a success that contributed US air support, Britain and Jordan. The attack, condemned by the UN and the western stationary, caused some thirty contuses and wounded, including a child in severe conditions.   The Islamic Republic claims to have hit an Israeli airbase > in the Negev desert but, according to Israeli armed forces, there were only "minimum infrastructure damage". The President of the United States, Joe Biden>, called the G7 for a "united diplomatic reaction" and called the Israeli premier, Benjamin Netanyahu>, inviting him not to respond to the attack. Washington does not want to be dragged into a wider conflict and is not convinced by Israel's warfare, sources of the American administration report. Egypt, which put its large army in alert state, and Saudi Arabia invited both parties to exercise the "maximum moderation". China expressed "profound concern". The Russian Deputy Permanent Representative to the United Nations said that "everything will depend on the next steps of the parties involved, or their choice not to carry out."     According to the New York Times, Israel will coordinate the response with allies. The damage caused by the attack was defined as "very small" and in all 36 cruise missiles and 185 drones would be thrown, with the support of regional allies such as Hezbollah, the Houthi and some Iraqi Shiite militias. The UN, at the request of Israel, convened an emergency meeting of the Security Council. The Iranian representative at the Palazzo di Vetro said he exercised the right to self-defense, following an attack on his territory. If it is difficult for Israel not to respond, the authorities of Tehran would be subjected to unsustainable internal pressure if they had not acted after the events of Damascus. The Iranian representation to the UN has in fact claimed to have acted according to the principle of self-defense.    < IDF, 300 drones and missiles from Iran. 99% affected outside Israel Israeli Armed Forces spokesman, Admiral Daniel Hagari, confirmed that 99% of the 300 missiles and drones launched by Iran against Israel at night were intercepted and shot down by air defense. In particular, he explained, 170 drones were launched, none of which arrived at the Israeli airspace: all have been destroyed out of the country's borders by Israel and its allies. Not even the 30 cruise missiles managed to enter the Israeli airspace: 25 were shot down by the Israeli Air Force. 120 ballistic missiles were launched, some of which circumvented the Israeli defences by striking Nevatim's air base in southern Israel: have caused only light damage and the base continues to be regularly operational. As for the few drones and missiles launched by Iraq and Yemen, no one has arrived at the airspace of the Jewish state. 
Cina deeply concerned: invites calm China expressed its "profound concern" after the Iranian attack on Israel in a note from the Beijing Foreign Ministry. "China expresses deep concern for the current worsening (of the situation) and invites interested parties to calm and moderation to avoid further escalation," reads in the statement.

The Russian diplomat at the UN: new acute crisis  

 'escalation of the conflict between Iran and Israel "may lead to a new acute crisis in the Middle East". He told Tass the Russian Deputy Permanent Representative to the United Nations, Dmitry Polyansky. "Everything depends on the next steps of the parties involved or their choice not to make them," said the diplomat. 

Hezbollah claims new rocket launch on Golan

Hezbollah claimed the launch of Katiuscia rocket mills on Israeli military positions on the heights of the Golan, which Tel Aviv took to Syria in the Six Day War of 1967. The Lebanese Shiite militia in previous hours had contributed to the Iranian attack and frequently launches bullets against the North of the Jewish State. 

Israel will coordinate the response with allies (Nyt)

Israel will coordinate its response to Iran's attack with its allies. The New York Times writes, citing well-informed sources. It is not specified to which allies would refer.  Israel evaluates as "relatively small" the damage caused by Tehran, which would launch against the territory of the Jewish State 185 drones and 36 cruise missiles.

Biden asks Netanyahu not to respond to the attack, Austin hears Gallant

The call ended between Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and US President Joe Biden, who asked the interlocutor to refrain from responding to the Iranian attack this night. Israel Hayom reports it. The conversation was confirmed by the White House, which will spread the details later. U.S. Secretary of Defense, Lloyd Austin, had a telephone conversation with the Israeli counterpart, Yoav Gallant, and asked him to warn the United States before any response to the Iranian attack this night. He told Cnn an American administration official. Gallant, according to Israeli sources, updated Austin on the trend of defensive operations and stressed that the Jewish state is ready for any new attack. US officials reported to the Nbc the fear of Washington that Tel Aviv responds to the Tehran attack without evaluating the consequences. 

Israel: residents may leave refuges The command of the Israeli armed forces has communicated to residents who can leave shelters, a sign that the Iranian attack could be considered concluded. The Times of Israel. Restrictions on assemblies and closure of schools and universities remain in force. According to emergency services, more than thirty people have requested medical assistance during operations. It is still unclear whether the girl who, according to the Tel Aviv army, was injured in the attack on the Negev Air Base is the 10-year-old person that emergency services defined in "critical conditions".  

Emergency meeting of the Security Council It was convened for 22 Italians on Sunday 14 April, at the request of Tel Aviv, an emergency meeting of the United Nations Security Council in relation to the Iranian attack on Israel. The Maltese presidency reports this.

Israel: more than 200 missiles and drones have been launched, "minimum damages" based

The Iranian attack on the Israeli air base in the Negev desert caused "minimum damage". The Israeli army spokesman, Daniel Hagari, said that the base reported "improved infrastructural damage" and that a girl was injured in the attack.
According to Hagari, Tehran launched over 200 bullets against the Jewish state and the Israeli military air force shot dozens of cruise missiles and drones. Most missiles, the spokesman added, were intercepted out of Israeli airspace by the Arrow air defense system, where the rest was shot down by the fighters. The attack, Hagari concluded, is still in progress. 

Iran, attacked an Israeli air base in the Negev  desert;

Iran claims to have attacked an Israeli air base in the Negev desert with Khaybar ballistic missiles. The Iranian agency Irna reports it. A spokesman for the Israeli army confirmed, stressing that  The Iranian attack on the Israeli air base in the Negev desert caused "minimum damage".

Israel, 99% of Iranian missiles and drones Israel intercepted the "99 percent" of Iran's raids and drones against its territory. Anonymous Tel Aviv officials reported this to Ynet, ensuring that a "decisive response will come soon".

Guterres, the region and the world cannot afford a war Antonio Guterres, Secretary-General of the United Nations, asked for immediate relief in the Middle East and stated in a statement: "They firmly condemn the serious escalation represented by the large-scale attack launched tonight against Israel by the Islamic Republic of Iran." The Secretary of the UN said that the region is likely to fall into a deeper and wider war on multiple fronts. "I have repeatedly stressed that neither the region nor the world can afford another war."  

Israel asks Guterres for an explicit condemnation of Iran Israel asked UN Secretary General Antonio Guterres to explicitly condemn Iran for the attack on its territory, first with drones and then with ballistic missiles. The Israeli ambassador to the UN, Gilad Erdan, who from the beginning of the war was very bellicose to Guterres, wrote on his account X a message to the Secretary-General: "Iran has violated the UN card (...) where are your words, where is your condemnation? The Secretary-General has not yet commented on the attack, which has already received condemnation messages from the United States, the European Union, France and Germany. <

  Israel, some wounded in the escape to refugee Some Israeli citizens were injured in fleeing to the shelters during the Iranian attack with drones and missiles. The Israeli emergency service reports it, according to Abc. At the moment we have no news of any death.

Abc, from Iran 150 missiles against Israel in two phases

There are 150 missiles launched by Iran against the Israeli territory, in two tranche from 80 and 70 missiles respectively. He told Abc an American official.

Biden, our support for Israel is unshakeable "I just met my national security team for an update on Iran's attacks against Israel. Our commitment to Israel's security against threats from Iran and its delegates is unwavering." He writes on X US President Joe Biden, posting a photo of the meeting in the Situation Room at the White House.

Tv, Lebanese antiaircraft in action above Beirut

The Lebanese anti-aircraft defenses launched several missiles in the skies above Beirut, presumably in response to Iranian bullets passing through its airspace to Israel, TeleLiban reported it. "Missili interceptors explode over the Beirut airport road," said the channel, referring that explosions could also be heard in different parts of the country, from east to south, perhaps triggered by other responses of the anti-aircraft defenses.

Medical sources, serious 10-year-old child in Israel  

A 10-year-old child is in serious condition after being injured in the South of Israel during the Iranian attack. It is reported by medical sources quoted by the Times of Israel, explaining that the child is native to a Bedouin town near Arad.

Damasco, dismissed an Israeli air strike at the airport The Syrian armed forces claim to have rejected an Israeli attack at the international airport of Damascus. The newspaper Al Watan reports it, relaunched by the agency Tass. Previously some Arab media had reported explosions in the sky of the Syrian capital. Different explosions in Damascus Several explosions were heard in Damascus, the capital of Syria. That's what the France Press could have done on the spot.

The map of the attack on Israel

< Intercepted 100 Iran drones outside Israel by the US and Great Britain More than 100 Iranian drones have already been intercepted by the United States and Great Britain outside Israeli airspace. It was reported on the Army radio by an Israeli defense officer on the Army radio, according to the Times of Israel.    < < < < < > > < > < > > < > < > < > < > < > < > > < > < > > < > > > > > > > > < > > > < > > > > > > > > < > > > > > > > > > > > > > < > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > < > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > >  

The EU condemns Iran's attack, "Escalation unprecedented"

The High Representative of the European Union for Foreign Affairs and Security, Josep Borrell, expressed "conflict condemnation" for the Iranian attack on Israel. "The EU strongly condemns the unacceptable Iranian attack on Israel," wrote Borrell on X, "this is an unprecedented escalation and a serious threat to regional security."

Iran to the USA: "By the conflict with Israel"

"If Israel commits another mistake, the response of Iran will be considerably more severe. It is a conflict between Iran and the Israeli rogue regime, from which the United States must stay away." It writes on X the permanent representation of Iran to the United Nations, stating that "the operation can be concluded". The appeal to Americans to stay away is written in a dull character.  

Iranian missiles will hit Golan and air base in Negev

The drones and missiles that Iran has launched against Israel will strike the Golan Heights - at the border between Israel, Lebanon, Jordan and Syria - and an Israeli air base in the Negev desert, in southern Israel. The New York Times reports it.

Crosetto: "The attack can cause a dramatic escalation"

"I receive constant updates and I follow with apprehension what is happening in the Middle East. They firmly condemn the attack of Iran and its allies against Israel, an unprecedented action that can cause dramatic escalation. We continue to work to avoid it." This is what the Minister of Defense wrote about "X", Guido Crosetto.    < Teheran: "It is the response to the aggression of the Zionist regime" "By virtue of Article 51 of the United Nations Charter on legitimate defence, Iran's military action is in response to the aggression of the Zionist regime against our diplomatic offices in Damascus." It writes on X the permanent representation of Iran to the United Nations. Egypt calls for "maximum moderation" The Egyptian Foreign Ministry expressed "profound concern" for the ongoing escalation between Iran and Israel and asked the parties to exercise the "maximum moderation". The agency Anadolu.  reports it;

Biden brings together the National Security Council The meeting began at the White House in the Situation Room, the emergency room, between the President of the United States, Joe Biden, and the National Security Advisors. Reuters mentions government sources. 

Hezbollah announces missile launch against Golan

The Lebanese pro-Iranian militia Hezbollah announced it launched missiles against the Golan, occupied by Israel.

From Yemen Houthi launch drones on Israel

The Houthi rebels of Yemen launched several drones against Israel in coordination with Iran, declared it the Ambrey security company, adding that the bullets were probably planned to reach Israel at the same time.   < "The pilotless air vehicles (UAV) would have been launched by the Huthi to Israel. The UAVs were launched in coordination with Iran," said the company. "The Israeli ports are considered potential objectives", added the society, which warned from " collateral damages" to the marine transport.  

Iran threatens those who open air space to support Israel Iran's Minister of Defense, Reza Ashtiani, warned that Tehran will respond firmly to any country "open its air space or territory for Israeli attacks against Iran". It is reported by the Iranian semi-official agency Mehr, relaunched by the Times of Israel. 

Iran and allies will launch 400-500 drones and missiles The American government believes that Iran will launch 400-500 drones and missiles from its territory and from countries such as Iraq, Syria and southern Lebanon, where Iranian militias or allies of Tehran are present. Abc News reports, specifying that most will be launched by Iran. 

France condemns Iran's attack on Israel France condemns Iran's attack on Israel, which "carrys its destabilizing actions to a new level", said Foreign Minister Stephane Sejourne on X.
"Deciding to take an unprecedented action, Iran is overcoming a new threshold in its destabilizing actions and runs the risk of military escalation," he wrote, adding that "France reaffirms its attachment to Israel's security and assures it its solidarity".

Sunak: "The Iranian attack is undesirable"

Britain condemns Iran's attack on Israel, said British Prime Minister Rishi Sunak in a statement published on X. "They firmly condemn the undesirable attack of the Iranian regime against Israel. Iran has once again shown that it is willing to sow chaos in its own courtyard," says.
Great Britain "will continue to defend the security of Israel and that of all our regional partners, including Jordan and Iraq." "With our allies, we are working urgently to stabilize the situation and prevent further escalation. Nobody wants to see more bloodshed."  

Back from Lebanon, sirens explained in kibbutz Israel

The alarm sirens resound in the community of Snir, in the North of Israel, due to an attack with rockets from Lebanon. The Times of Israel reports it, recalling that Hezbollah carried out several attacks in Northern Israel during the Gaza Strip war. There is no immediate news of damage or injury in the attack.

Egyptian air defence at maximum alert

The Egyptian Air Defense is in maximum alert after the attack launched by Iran with drones and cruise missiles against Israel. Local media report it.

Khamenei, Israel will be punished "The wicked Zionist regime will be punished," said the Iranian supreme leader Sayyid Ali Khamenei on X.   < Biden in the room. The attack will last hours
